records.sap_reference: S4TWL - SRM Product Master (S4TWL item 5.1.7, SAP Note 2267297)
S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: Maintain your indirect activity allocation cycles in KSC1 to KSC3 from now on. The cost-centre transactions take a business process as sender or receiver, which makes the ABC-specific codes redundant, and parallel ABC on delta versions is gone entirely.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: Run indirect activity allocation through KSC5 instead. S/4HANA drops the ABC-specific code because the cost-centre equivalent handles business processes directly, and only operational version 0 ABC survives the move.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: For activity price calculation, switch to KSII. It carries business processes as sender and receiver, so the ABC version of the transaction is retired, along with delta versions and parallel Activity-Based Costing.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: Assessment cycle maintenance now happens in KSU1 to KSU3. SAP maps these directly from the ABC codes, since the cost-centre transactions cover business processes too, and note that delta-version ABC does not exist in S/4HANA.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: Use KSU5 to run assessments going forward. The ABC-specific transaction falls away because the standard one covers business processes, and only integrated ABC in version 0 continues.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - Construction Progress Report and Valuation (S4TWL item 10.1.31, SAP Note 2267289)
S4TWL - GENERAL COST OBJECTS AND COST OBJECT (S4TWL item 6.5.3, SAP Note 2270411)
records.delta_note: Template allocation for cost objects ends here. General cost objects do not exist in S/4HANA, so this work shifts across to product cost collectors or internal orders.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - GENERAL COST OBJECTS AND COST OBJECT (S4TWL item 6.5.3, SAP Note 2270411)
records.delta_note: The collective template allocation run for cost objects has no home in S/4HANA, because general cost objects themselves are gone. Rebuild the process on product cost collectors or internal orders.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: Distribution cycle maintenance moves to KSV1 to KSV3. The cost-centre codes now accept business processes as sender or receiver, so these ABC variants are redundant, and parallel ABC on delta versions is not supported at all.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: Run distributions with KSV5 in S/4HANA. It replaces the ABC-specific code directly, and if you were using parallel Activity-Based Costing on delta versions, be aware delta versions are gone entirely.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - CASH MANAGEMENT - GENERAL (S4TWL item 6.11.17, SAP Note 2270400)

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - CASH MANAGEMENT - GENERAL (S4TWL item 6.11.17, SAP Note 2270400)
records.delta_note: The classic cash management scope that FF-5 belonged to ends at conversion. You pick up the equivalent memo record and planning functions in SAP S/4HANA Finance for cash management.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - CASH MANAGEMENT - GENERAL (S4TWL item 6.11.17, SAP Note 2270400)
records.delta_note: FF-6 retires with the rest of the old FF* transactions. The successor is the new cash management in S/4HANA Finance, and it is a reconfiguration rather than a migration - plan for setup work, not a data move.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - CASH MANAGEMENT - GENERAL (S4TWL item 6.11.17, SAP Note 2270400)
records.delta_note: Memo records and planning move house. FF69 is part of the classic cash management set that is not delivered with S/4HANA, and the new cash management covers the equivalent ground.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - CASH MANAGEMENT - GENERAL (S4TWL item 6.11.17, SAP Note 2270400)
records.delta_note: Another classic cash management code that stops at the S/4HANA boundary. FF70's territory is reconfigured inside SAP S/4HANA Finance for cash management.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - CASH MANAGEMENT - GENERAL (S4TWL item 6.11.17, SAP Note 2270400)
records.delta_note: Out of scope in S/4HANA. FF71 goes with classic Cash and Liquidity Management, and its functions are configured in the new cash management instead.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - CASH MANAGEMENT - GENERAL (S4TWL item 6.11.17, SAP Note 2270400)
records.delta_note: Ends with the classic FF* set. What FF72 did is covered by SAP S/4HANA Finance for cash management once that is implemented, and Bank Account Management becomes a core workstream of the conversion.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - CASH MANAGEMENT - GENERAL (S4TWL item 6.11.17, SAP Note 2270400)
records.delta_note: Your cash position moves to new screens. FF7A is superseded by the Cash Position apps and, from release 1809, the consolidated Cash Flow Analyzer app.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - CASH MANAGEMENT - GENERAL (S4TWL item 6.11.17, SAP Note 2270400)
records.delta_note: Liquidity forecasting leaves FF7B behind. The Liquidity Forecast apps take over on the new cash management data model, with the Cash Flow Analyzer arriving later as the consolidated view.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - CASH MANAGEMENT - GENERAL (S4TWL item 6.11.17, SAP Note 2270400)

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - CASH MANAGEMENT - GENERAL (S4TWL item 6.11.17, SAP Note 2270400)
records.delta_note: Retired with classic Cash and Liquidity Management. FFTL has no direct carry-over, and the equivalent function is set up in SAP S/4HANA Finance for cash management.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - CASH MANAGEMENT - GENERAL (S4TWL item 6.11.17, SAP Note 2270400)
records.delta_note: Classic cash management takes FFZK with it. Plan for the equivalent capability inside the new cash management, alongside the Bank Account Management implementation the conversion requires.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - CASH MANAGEMENT - GENERAL (S4TWL item 6.11.17, SAP Note 2270400)

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: Keep using KB11N as you do today. It appears in the simplification item only as the covering transaction for the retired parallel ABC variant KB11NP, not as a removal.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: The parallel ABC variant KB11NP goes away. Post through KB11N instead, where the business process is a standard account assignment, since delta-version ABC does not exist in S/4HANA.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: No action needed on your side for KB13N. It appears in the Simplification List only as the covering transaction for the retired KB13NP parallel ABC variant, and continues unchanged itself.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: KB13NP disappears together with parallel Activity-Based Costing on delta versions. Its work folds into KB13N, where the business process is handled as a standard account assignment.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: You will find KB14N in the Simplification List, but only because it absorbs its retired parallel ABC variant KB14NP. The transaction itself stays current in S/4HANA.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: This parallel ABC posting variant is retired. Use KB14N, which treats the business process as a normal account assignment, because delta versions and parallel ABC are gone from S/4HANA.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: KB21N carries on unchanged. Its Simplification List entry exists only to cover the removal of the parallel ABC variant KB21NP, so treat the listing as informational.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: Posting through the KB21NP parallel ABC variant ends in S/4HANA. Switch to KB21N, where the business process is simply a standard account assignment on the posting.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: There is no change to KB23N itself. The Simplification List names it as the successor that covers the retired KB23NP variant, and only the parallel ABC side disappears.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: With delta versions gone entirely, the KB23NP variant has no purpose left. Its postings move to KB23N as standard account assignments on the business process.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: KB24N remains a current transaction. It is listed in the simplification item purely as cover for KB24NP, the parallel ABC variant that is being retired.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - ACTIVITY-BASED COSTING (S4TWL item 6.5.2, SAP Note 2270408)
records.delta_note: One less code to remember here. KB24NP is retired with parallel Activity-Based Costing, and KB24N handles the same posting with the business process as a standard account assignment.

records.sap_reference: S4TWL - Profitability Analysis (S4TWL item 6.5.11, SAP Note 2349278)
records.delta_note: Periodic valuation as KE27 ran it belongs to costing-based CO-PA and has no counterpart in Margin Analysis. SAP's direction is to cover the requirement in Margin Analysis, paired with event-based revenue recognition, and keep costing-based CO-PA switched off where you can.

records.delta_note: Your day-to-day vendor line item list keeps working after conversion, but SAP's direction is the Manage Supplier Line Items app (SAP now says Supplier, not Vendor). The app swaps the classic selection screen for filter bars and builds in actions like blocking and paying, and your saved FBL1N layouts will not come across, so plan to rebuild them.

records.replacement: Display Line Items in General Ledger (Fiori app)
records.delta_note: You can keep running FBL3N, though SAP treats the Display Line Items in General Ledger app as the way forward. It reads the Universal Journal directly, and you filter your way to line items with drill-through to journal entries rather than working through the classic list variants.

Customer line items still open the way you are used to, but the Manage Customer Line Items app is where SAP is heading, with dispute, correspondence and payment actions built into the list. Your saved layouts and any user exits around the classic list need checking again after conversion.

Nothing changes in how FB60 posts, and no simplification item removes it. If you move to the Create Supplier Invoice apps you gain built-in duplicate checks and attachment handling, but you can stay in the GUI transaction for now.

FB70 carries on in S/4HANA exactly as before. SAP's preferred front end for customer invoices is the Create Outgoing Invoices app, which sits on the same posting core, so switching is about screens rather than accounting.
Your payment run is safe. F110 keeps the same engine in S/4HANA, and the Manage Automatic Payments app simply gives you a guided run monitor and easier exception handling on top of it.

Balances in FS10N now come through compatibility views rather than the old totals tables, so figures and performance can behave slightly differently. SAP steers you towards the Display G/L Account Balances and Trial Balance apps, which read the Universal Journal natively.

You can still check supplier balances in FK10N, with the numbers now coming from the Universal Journal instead of index tables. The Display Supplier Balances app is the designated successor and lets you drill straight down to line items and journal entries.

Customer balances keep opening in FD10N under compatibility scope, and no removal item applies to the transaction. SAP's go-forward screen is the Display Customer Balances app, which reads the Universal Journal and drills through to receivables line items.

KE30 reports still run against costing-based CO-PA, but SAP's strategic answer for profitability is Margin Analysis in the Universal Journal. Your Report Painter reports on the CE1 to CE4 tables do not translate one to one, so expect the reporting layer to be rebuilt rather than migrated.

Line items for costing-based CO-PA stay visible in KE24. Account-based figures live in the Universal Journal and surface through journal entry and margin apps instead, so if you run both you will be reporting in two places.

Invoice verification stays in MIRO, which no simplification item touches. The Create Supplier Invoice app is SAP's strategic front end over the same core, so treat the app as an option rather than a deadline.

You keep creating purchase orders in ME21N as before. The visible change is at the edges, where output for new documents shifts from NAST messages towards BRF+ based output, and the Manage Purchase Orders apps are the target experience.

MB51 still lists your material documents, now reading them from the MATDOC table. Re-check any custom layouts or queries built on the old MKPF and MSEG model, and note the Material Documents Overview app is the strategic successor.

records.replacement: Display Line Items in General Ledger (Fiori app)
records.delta_note: You can keep running FAGLL03 after conversion - it now reads the Universal Journal through compatibility views, so figures still tie up even though the old totals tables are gone underneath. SAP's direction is the Display Line Items in General Ledger app, the same successor it names for FBL3N, with filter bars in place of the classic selection screen.

FAGLB03 still displays your ledger balances, but like FS10N it is fed through compatibility views rather than the old totals tables, so drill-down behaviour and performance can differ. The Display G/L Account Balances app reads the Universal Journal natively and is where SAP is steering balance display.

records.replacement: Manage G/L Account Master Data (Fiori app)
records.delta_note: FS00 keeps working, but the master data behind it has changed shape: cost elements are now G/L accounts, so the account type field decides whether an account behaves as a primary or secondary cost element. If you used to maintain cost elements in KA01/KA02, that work now happens here (or in the Manage G/L Account Master Data app).

Manual journal posting in FB50 carries on as before. The Post General Journal Entries app is SAP's preferred route - it adds spreadsheet-style entry, attachments on the entry itself, and (where your firm switches it on) approval workflow before posting.

FB03 still displays documents, now drawn from the Universal Journal. The Manage Journal Entries app covers the same ground plus search, reversal and attachment handling in one place, and is where SAP points anyone who lives in document display all day.

Manual outgoing payments in F-53 still work the way you know. The Post Outgoing Payments app is the successor SAP names - one screen for bank data, open-item selection and posting, instead of the classic multi-screen flow. Your F110 payment runs are unaffected either way.

F-28 keeps posting customer receipts as before. SAP's direction is the Post Incoming Payments app, which pulls open-item matching onto one screen; cash application teams on high volumes may also meet Cash Application machine-matching, which is a separate licensed option.

records.replacement: Clear G/L Accounts - Manual Clearing (Fiori app)
records.delta_note: Account clearing in F-03 carries on unchanged at the desk. The Clear G/L Accounts app does the same job with filterable open-item lists, and automatic clearing (F.13) also survives - so your period-end clearing routine transfers, even if the screens you do it in eventually change.

records.replacement: Display Line Items - Cost Accounting (Fiori app)
records.delta_note: KSB1 still lists your cost centre actuals, now read from the Universal Journal through a compatibility view - totals should agree with the old world, but exotic layout variants are worth re-testing. The Display Line Items - Cost Accounting app is the native successor and covers cost centres and orders in one place.

records.replacement: Display Line Items - Cost Accounting (Fiori app)
records.delta_note: Order line items in KOB1 keep working, fed by the Universal Journal through a compatibility view rather than the old CO line-item tables. As with KSB1, the Display Line Items - Cost Accounting app is the go-forward tool, so anyone reporting on internal orders should expect to end up there over time.
